Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a type of streaming service product available for sale (e.g., 'Netflix Premium Account').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The common name of the streaming service product, e.g., 'Netflix Premium', 'Disney+ Standard'.

description.description: A detailed description of the product, including features and benefits.

features.description: A list of key features included with this product, e.g., ['4K HDR', '4 Screens'].

basePrice.description: The base price for one unit of this product type.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the product is currently visible and available for purchase on the storefront.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was last updated.

StockItem.title: StockItem
StockItem.description: Represents an individual streaming service account credential available in stock.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StockItem entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product this stock item belongs to. (Relationship: Product 1:N StockItem)

credentials.description: The actual account credentials (e.g., username:password or access key). This data must be handled securely by the application layer.

status.description: The current status of the stock item (e.g., 'available', 'sold', 'delivered', 'revoked').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the stock item was added to inventory.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the stock item record was last updated.

deliveredAt.description: Timestamp when this specific stock item was delivered to a customer.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's purchase transaction, initiated via guest checkout.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

customerEmail.description: The email address of the customer who placed the order.

customerName.description: The name of the customer who placed the order (optional for guest checkout).

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including all items and taxes.

currency.description: The currency code for the totalAmount (e.g., 'BRL', 'USD').

status.description: The current processing status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'processing', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'refunded').

paymentIntentId.description: Reference ID from the external payment gateway for this transaction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order record was last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific product ordered within a customer's order, linking to a particular fulfilled stock item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product type that was ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

stockItemId.description: Reference to the specific StockItem (account credential) that was delivered to fulfill this order item. This is populated once the item is assigned/delivered. (Relationship: StockItem 1:1 OrderItem)

priceAtPurchase.description: The price of this specific item at the moment it was added to the order.

status.description: The fulfillment status of this individual order item (e.g., 'pending delivery', 'delivered', 'refunded').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order item record was last updated.

definition.description: Catalog of streaming service products available for sale. Publicly readable for display on the storefront. Admin writable for product management.

params.name: productId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Product document.

definition.description: Specific streaming account credentials available in inventory for a given product. This collection is for admin-only access due to sensitive credential information. Includes denormalized 'productId' for direct context.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Product.

params.name: stockItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the StockItem document.

definition.description: Customer purchase transactions initiated via guest checkout. Includes denormalized 'customerAuthId' (Firebase anonymous UID) for secure customer-specific access and 'customerEmail' for identification. Admin users can access all orders.

params.name: orderId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Order document.

definition.description: Individual product items within a customer's order. Includes denormalized 'customerAuthId' (from the parent Order) for customer-specific access. Admin users can access all order items.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem document.

definition.description: Stores administrator roles. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{uid}` grants admin privileges to that UID. Used for Database Access Control (DBAC).

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to support a streaming service sales platform with public product listings, guest checkout, and administrative management. It prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable security rules, adhering strictly to the core design principles and mandates.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization:**

1.  **Product (`/products/{productId}`):** This collection is publicly readable, meaning no specific authorization context (like an `ownerId`) is needed for read operations by general users. Administrative writes are controlled via a dedicated `/roles_admin` collection, ensuring admin access is independent of product document content. Since there's no `get()` dependency on a parent for authorization, this path naturally adheres to the principle.
2.  **StockItem (`/products/{productId}/stockItems/{stockItemId}`):** Access to stock items (credentials) is strictly limited to administrators. No customer-specific authorization is required. The `productId` is embedded in the path, providing immediate context without requiring parent `get()` calls for rules. The collection's uniform security posture (admin-only) prevents hierarchical authorization issues.
3.  **Order (`/orders/{orderId}`):** For guest checkout, the `customerAuthId` (Firebase Anonymous Authentication UID) is **denormalized** directly into each `Order` document. This allows security rules to directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.customerAuthId` for customer-specific order access, completely eliminating `get()` calls to a non-existent parent user document or a separate authorization collection for individual orders. Administrative access is handled by checking for existence in the `/roles_admin` collection, also avoiding `get()` dependencies.
4.  **OrderItem (`/orders/{orderId}/orderItems/{orderItemId}`):** Similar to `Order`, the `customerAuthId` from the parent order is **denormalized** into each `OrderItem` document. This ensures that rules can independently verify `request.auth.uid` against `resource.data.customerAuthId` for each item, maintaining authorization independence and allowing atomic operations across order and order item documents without rule failures due to `get()` calls to the parent `Order`.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) & Structural Segregation:**

1.  **Public Product Listing (`/products`):** The entire `/products` collection is designed for public `list` operations, directly fulfilling the QAP. Users can retrieve the full catalog without security rules acting as filters on individual documents.
2.  **Admin Stock Management (`/products/{productId}/stockItems`):** This subcollection is dedicated to sensitive stock items, accessible only by administrators. Admins can `list` all stock items for a given product without rules filtering out specific documents, as all documents in this collection share the same security posture.
3.  **Order Tracking (`/orders` and `/orders/{orderId}/orderItems`):** Administrators can `list` all documents in these collections, fulfilling the QAP for admin users. For guest customers, `list` operations on `/orders` (or subcollections) are designed such that if an anonymous user requests their orders, a rule can enforce `request.auth.uid == resource.data.customerAuthId`. While this technically acts as a filter on `list` operations, it's a standard and optimized pattern for an authenticated user to access *their own* data, leveraging Firestore's indexing capabilities. Crucially, individual `get()` operations for a specific order by a customer will directly use the denormalized `customerAuthId` in the document, avoiding problematic `get()` filters.

**DBAC and Predictability:**

*   **Global Roles (`/roles_admin/{adminUid}`):** Administrator roles are managed via a dedicated top-level collection where the document ID is the `uid` of the administrator. The existence of a document signifies an admin role, allowing simple and debuggable `exists()` checks in security rules for administrative actions across any collection. This adheres to the DBAC principle by storing roles in the database, relying solely on `request.auth.uid` for role verification.
*   Consistent naming conventions like `productId`, `orderId`, `customerAuthId`, `status`, `createdAt`, and `updatedAt` are used throughout, ensuring clarity and predictability for developers and for writing concise, understandable security rules.
